tool_calls.Arguments: {"keystrokes": "python3 << 'PYEOF'\\nimport binascii\\n\\ntarget_crc = 0xb0725dc4\\n\\n# The zip says uncompressed size is 33 bytes\\n# Let's check the 33 bytes we extracted from the local file header\\nwith open('/app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat', 'rb') as f:\\n    data = f.read()\\n\\n# The 33 bytes of file data at offset 1048652\\nfile_data = data[1048652:1048652+33]\\nprint(f'Extracted data ({len(file_data)} bytes):')\\nprint(f'Hex: {file_data.hex()}')\\nprint(f'Raw: {repr(file_data)}')\\n\\ncrc = binascii.crc32(file_data) & 0xffffffff\\nprint(f'\\\\nCRC of extracted data: {crc:#x} (target: {target_crc:#x})')\\nprint(f'Match: {crc == target_crc}')\\n\\n# Now extract from central directory offset\\n# The central directory at 3145741\\n# Let's check the central directory entry which might point to the correct data\\nprint(f'\\\\n--- Checking central directory ---')\\ncd_offset = 3145741\\n# Central directory header is 46 bytes + filename (18) + extra (24) + comment (0)\\n# The \"local header offset\" is at bytes 42-45 of the central directory entry\\nimport struct\\nlocal_offset = struct.unpack('<I', data[cd_offset+42:cd_offset+46])[0]\\nprint(f'Local header offset from central dir: {local_offset}')\\n# But wait, this is the offset within the zip file, not the .dat file\\n# The zip started at 1048576, so the local offset relative to .dat would be 1048576 + local_offset?\\n# Actually the central directory stores local header offset relative to the start of the archive\\nprint(f'This would be at .dat offset: {1048576 + local_offset}')\\nPYEOF\\n", "duration": 0.3}
